title: `--tty` merges stdin and stderr
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Using `--tty` merges stdout and stderr of the command or `ENTRYPOINT`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

 1. Build this `Dockerfile`:

        FROM fedora:30
        RUN { \
                echo "#!/bin/bash"; \
                echo "echo i am output; echo i am error >&2"; \
            }> /bin/test_oe
        RUN chmod +x /bin/test_oe
        ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/test_oe"]

 2. `podman run --rm -t $IMAGE_ID` 1>1 2>2

 3. `head 1 2`

**Describe the results you received:**

    ==> 1 <==
    i am output
    i am error

    ==> 2 <==


**Describe the results you expected:**

    ==> 1 <==
    i am output

    ==> 2 <==
    i am error

(It's what I get without `-t`).


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I think `ENTRYPOINT` is not important in the reproducer; one would have
same problem without it and with running `test_oe` directly.

The use case is that my app generates data that needs to be parsed, so merging with
stderr (where it can post errors/info/debug) corrupts it.

Without `-t` it works mostly fine (save for aesthetics) but from what I've seen in
most of the docs, running with `-t` seems to be extremely common so I have to assume
that my users will use `-t`.


**Output of `podman version`:**

    Version:            1.6.2
    RemoteAPI Version:  1
    Go Version:         go1.12.10
    OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

          host_id: 1738400

      MemFree: 2604322816

      OCIRuntime:

        version: |-
          runc version 1.0.0-rc9+dev
          commit: 0840802d505e28d3adcbc7a275cbeadbdf64ddec
          spec: 1.0.1-dev
      SwapFree: 7786983424

        Version: |-
          slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.2
          commit: 19d199a6ca424fcf9516320a327cedad85cf4dfb
      uptime: 10h 40m 25.19s (Approximately 0.42 days)

comments.body:
unfortunately there is no way to understand whether the output on the tty is generated through stdout or stderr on the other end.

As you said, if you need to filter stdout/stderr then you need to drop `--tty`.
